description_long: This is a classic Western-style motel complex featuring two main rectangular buildings set on a sandy lot with sparse desert landscaping, including several cacti. The left building has a prominent wooden porch with posts, a sign reading 'SALOON', and a blue-gray facade with large windows and a central entrance. The right building, labeled 'HOTEL', has a slightly taller, brown wooden facade with a covered entryway supported by columns and a sign above the entrance. Both structures have flat, slightly sloped roofs and wooden siding, evoking a rustic, frontier-era aesthetic. The buildings are separated by a small alley and are surrounded by a dirt path, suggesting easy access for vehicles or pedestrians. The overall scene is reminiscent of a small-town main street in the American Old West.
description: A Western-style motel complex with two wooden buildings, covered porches, and desert landscaping, evoking a frontier-era main street.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the 'SALOON' and 'HOTEL' facades, covered porches, and cacti flanking the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the buildings, the wooden siding, and the alley between the two structures.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of both buildings, with less architectural detail and no signage visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the length of the porch and the arrangement of the cacti along the sandy lot.
